aliases: World of Warcraft: The War Within

config_files.path_note: Default Blizzard install path. Users who changed install location during Battle.net setup will have a different root. The WTF\ folder and Config.wtf are created on first game launch.
config_files.notes: Uses 'SET key "value"' syntax. The game rewrites this file on exit. Edit only while WoW is fully closed and Battle.net launcher is not actively patching.

variants.name: WoW Classic / Season of Discovery / Hardcore
variants.note: Separate Config.wtf for Classic clients. All keys in this profile apply to Classic as well, though graphicsSSAO and some Ultra+ settings may not be available in the Classic renderer.

variants.name: WoW PTR / Beta
variants.note: PTR has its own Config.wtf. Settings do not sync between PTR and Retail.

keys.notes: Ultra+ (5) requires 8 GB+ VRAM. In densely populated capital cities and outdoor raid entrances, texture streaming stutter is common with 6 GB VRAM at Ultra+. Use High (3) as a safe ceiling for 6-8 GB cards.

The single most impactful setting for raid and large-group performance. In Mythic raid progression, Low (0) can recover 20-40% GPU frame time versus Ultra. Ultra (4) is only appropriate for solo/open-world content.

keys.effect: World render distance for terrain, characters, and objects: 0=low (426m) through 5=ultra (2133m).
keys.notes: Major CPU cost driver in open-world zones. The War Within underground zones (Khaz Algar) and Dragonflight outdoor zones with high mob density benefit significantly from reducing this on CPU-limited hardware.

keys.effect: Ground clutter density (grass, small foliage): 0=low through 4=ultra.
Water and liquid surface quality: 0=low through 4=ultra.
Maximum frame rate when the game window is focused.
keys.notes: WoW ships uncapped. Capping 10 above monitor Hz (e.g. 170 for 165 Hz) prevents unnecessary GPU heat in low-complexity scenes.

keys.effect: Maximum frame rate when the game window is unfocused or minimised.
keys.notes: Default is uncapped. WoW is commonly left running during breaks, queues, and AFK. Capping background FPS to 30 significantly reduces idle GPU and CPU power draw.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: DLSS Quality on NVIDIA RTX hardware. FSR Quality on AMD. WoW added native DLSS 3.5 support in Patch 10.2 (Dragonflight) and DLSS 4 in Patch 11.0 (The War Within).
manual_only_settings.reason: Upscaler selection is only configurable via the in-game Advanced Graphics menu.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: Set a dedicated reduced-quality preset for raid instances. WoW's Raid Preset system (added in Dragonflight) automatically applies a separate graphics profile when entering raid zones.
manual_only_settings.reason: Separates graphics quality between solo/world content and group content where performance is critical. Configured entirely in-game.

notes: WoW Retail and WoW Classic maintain completely separate Config.wtf files. Changes to one do not affect the other.

The Raid Graphics Preset feature (Dragonflight patch 10.0+) automatically applies a lower graphics profile in raid instances. This is the recommended approach for players who want high quality in solo content and maximum performance in raids.
Config.wtf is recreated from defaults if deleted — this can be used to reset all graphics settings to factory state.
The WTF\ directory also contains per-character and per-account addon settings. Config.wtf is the global client configuration only.
Several graphicsSpellDensity reductions occurred silently in patches — if a user is on a pre-Dragonflight guide, values may reference different numeric scales.
